subject: add filebeat to k8s log demo
commit_message:
add filebeat to k8s log demo

Change-Id: I1c229a9e5f7c9379d8d9f9fdf724908f083925d9
Issue-ID: LOG-137
Signed-off-by: Michael O'Brien <frank.obrien@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

WIP - do not merge

Patch Set 1:

logs getting to filebeat via emptyDir but not appearing in logstash yet - triaging

Patch Set 1:

checked vid - and I am missing metrics for the harvester like
2018/07/23 14:47:24.463556 metrics.go:39: INFO Non-zero metrics in the last 30s: filebeat.harvester.open_files=5 filebeat.harvester.running=5 filebeat.harvester.started=5 libbeat.logstash.call_count.PublishEvents=1 libbeat.logstash.publish.read_bytes=42 libbeat.logstash.publish.write_bytes=22495 libbeat.logstash.published_and_acked_events=298 libbeat.publisher.published_events=298 publish.events=303 registrar.states.current=5 registrar.states.update=303 registrar.writes=2

Patch Set 1:

found the issue - no /var/log/onap on the filebeat (only on the main container)
bash-4.2$ ls /var/log/onap
ls: cannot access /var/log/onap: No such file or directory

Patch Set 2:

moved config down to lower values.yaml

Patch Set 2:

kibana working now - see logs for the demo in https://jira.onap.org/secure/attachment/12044/Screenshot%202018-07-23%2012.24.28.png

Patch Set 3:

will run in separate release but same onap namespace to verify dns still works across two helm deployment - liasing with https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/56445/5

Patch Set 3:

going to move filebeat back up to common - above the subchart

Patch Set 8:

for running two deployments connected by namespace - was able to run a logdemo chart from the loggging-analytics repo in a different release and connect to log-ls.onap running in the oom repo - like you mentioned as long as the two deployments share the namespace - so I will update my gerrit review with a copy of your Makefile https://wiki.onap.org/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=28378955&refresh=1532705478844&focusedCommentId=38114122&refresh=1532705499406#comment-38114122

Patch Set 8:

# prereq
- Makefile in k8s root - copied/modified-parent-chart from https://git.onap.org/oom/tree/kubernetes/Makefile
- oom and logging-analytics cloned
# install onap log
ubuntu@ip-172-31-54-73:~/_dev/log-137-57171/oom/kubernetes$ sudo helm delete --purge onap
ubuntu@ip-172-31-54-73:~/_dev/log-137-57171/oom/kubernetes$ sudo make all
ubuntu@ip-172-31-54-73:~/_dev/log-137-57171/oom/kubernetes$ sudo helm install local/onap -n onap --namespace onap -f onap/resources/environments/disable-allcharts.yaml --set log.enabled=false
ubuntu@ip-172-31-54-73:~/_dev/log-137-57171/oom/kubernetes$ sudo helm upgrade -i onap local/onap --namespace onap -f onap/resources/environments/disable-allcharts.yaml --set log.enabled=true
 
# install logdemo
ubuntu@ip-172-31-54-73:~/_dev/log-137-57171/logging-analytics/reference/logging-kubernetes$ sudo helm delete --purge logdemonode
ubuntu@ip-172-31-54-73:~/_dev/log-137-57171/logging-analytics/reference/logging-kubernetes$ sudo make all
ubuntu@ip-172-31-54-73:~/_dev/log-137-57171/logging-analytics/reference/logging-kubernetes$ sudo helm install local/logdemonode -n logdemonode --namespace onap --set logdemonode.enabled=true
 
# rebuild after code change
ubuntu@ip-172-31-54-73:~/_dev/log-137-57171/logging-analytics/reference/logging-kubernetes$ sudo helm upgrade -i logdemonode local/logdemonode --namespace onap --set logdemonode.enabled=false

Patch Set 10:

This single pod exercises both the slf4j library from Luke and the AOP wrapper in a docker container with associated pod that logs to log-ls in the same namespaces - next steps is to abstract out the AOP wrapper into its own jar and finish the final logback.xml and associated ELK indexing changes
